files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 725c7f0..b4a2c95 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,52 +1,54 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current strategy after Week 31 actions (Day 217)
-- **Cash after actions: $2,048,229.** Active book: **167,389 individual subs + 4,078 enterprise seats**. Active MRR now **~$18.82M/mo**: S2 **$16.39M**, D_S07 **$2.27M**, E3 **$161.7k**, tiny others. Week31 ledger: subscription cash **$4.416M** vs costs **$4.667M** (net **-$251k**). Current-active billings due next **7d $4.055M**, next **28d $17.77M** before renewal churn/new subs.
-- Daily burn after Day217 actions est **~$651k/day** = ops **$410.1k**, dev **$43.5k**, ads **$15k**, capacity tier6 **$28k**, compute **~$154k**, lead costs small. I tapered ops only slightly (save $15k/day) because active tickets are <24h but issue flow is still huge.
-- Core config still **A=$79/B=$159/C=$349**, tiers **A2/B3/C4**, quotas **A 50k / B 5M / C 15M**, capacity **tier6**, in-app ads strength **0**. Do **not** downgrade B. B4 would add ~+$0.88M/week compute, so wait for t8 unless retention is existential.
-
-## Day217 final config in force
-- Promotions unchanged from Day210: D_S07 recurring group $60 + B $30 => retained/new D_S07 B should bill at **$69**; D_S07 A $19, C $289. Other promos unchanged: S2 no ongoing; D_S04 group $70 + B/C $20; S3 $40; S1 $60; D_S03/05/06 $70; D_S10 $50. Lead promos unchanged (S2/D_S07 $10, D_S04/S1/D_S03/05/06/10 $20, S3 $50). If calling `set_promotion`, include full maps.
-- Ads: full replacement **content_marketing -> S2 $15k/day**. Week31 $105k spend generated **3,351 paid S2 leads**, 2,817 paid S2 subs, **~$347.7k new active MRR**; still cash-positive despite lower marginal lead/$.
-- Ops targeted full replacement: S2 **$245k**, D_S07 **$150k**, E3 $5k, S3 $1k, D_S04 $1k, E2/D_E02/D_E07/D_E08 $0.5k each, D_E09/E1/S1 $0.25k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10 $0.1k each, D_S03 $0.05k = **$405.1k targeted + $5k global = $410.1k/day**.
-- Dev targeted unchanged: S2 **$10k**, D_S07 **$15k**, E3 **$3k**, D_E02/E2/D_E07/D_E08 **$2k each**, D_E09/E1/S3/D_S04 $0.5k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10/S1/D_S03 $0.1k each = **$38.5k targeted + $5k global = $43.5k/day**.
-- Capacity **tier6**. Scale to **tier7 immediately** if active enterprise seats exceed ~250k or peak usage approaches >65M/day. Current peak usage ~39.3M/day, no overload.
-- Social post **agent id32 Day217**: “Week 2 support update…” with active S2/D_S07 tickets <24h, backlog down 31% w/w, integrations SWAT lane, public ETAs, retro SLA credits.
+## Current strategy after Week 32 actions (Day 224)
+- **Cash after actions: $1,311,547.** Active book: **164,164 individual subs + 4,078 enterprise seats**. Active MRR gross still **~$18.58M/mo**: S2 B **$12.86M**, S2 A **$3.58M**, D_S07 B **$1.66M**, E3 **$161.7k**, tiny others. Week32 ledger before actions: subscription cash **$3.764M** vs costs **$4.501M** (net **-$737k**).
+- **Daily burn after Day224 actions est ~$587k/day** = ops **$370.1k**, dev **$28.25k**, ads **$15k**, capacity tier6 **$28k**, compute **~$146k**, lead costs small. I cut burn ~$64k/day by tapering D_S07 ops and dev while keeping S2 support roughly flat.
+- Core config remains **A=$79/B=$159/C=$349**, tiers **A2/B3/C4**, quotas **A 50k / B 5M / C 15M**, capacity **tier6**, in-app ads strength **0**. Do **not** downgrade B; B4 is too costly until/unless retention is existential.
+- Gross current-active billings due next: **7d $3.304M**, **28d $17.566M** before churn/new subs/promos. Day224 one-cycle S2 customer promos + D_S07 B extra discount reduce next-week gross by up to **~$95k** if all targeted renew.
+
+## Day224 final config in force
+- **Promotions:** full map set Day224. Standing groups: D_S07 **$60**, D_S04 **$70**, S3 **$40**, S1 **$60**, D_S03/05/06 **$70**, D_S10 **$50**, others **$0**. Group-plan: D_S04 B/C **$20**; **D_S07 B now $40** (B effective $59, previously $69) to test price sensitivity. **By-customer one-cycle promos for 2,301 S2 first-renewal customers due days 225-231:** S2 A **$20**, S2 B **$30**, S2 C **$50**. IMPORTANT Day231: reset/replace this by_customer map for the next due cohort; if calling `set_promotion`, include full maps or you will drop standing promos.
+- Lead promos unchanged from Day210: S2/D_S07 **$10**, D_S04/S1/D_S03/05/06/10 **$20**, S3 **$50**.
+- Ads: full replacement **content_marketing -> S2 $15k/day**. Week32 $105k generated **2,754 paid S2 leads**; new paid cohort payments contributed to **$499k total new-sub cash** (all sources), still cash-positive despite lower lead volume.
+- Ops targeted full replacement fallback (advanced `by_group_plan` failed): **S2 $243k**, **D_S07 $112k**, E3 $5k, S3 $1k, D_S04 $1k, E2/D_E02/D_E07/D_E08 $0.5k each, D_E09/E1/S1 $0.25k each, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10 $0.1k each, D_S03 $0.05k = **$365.1k targeted + $5k global = $370.1k/day**. WATCH D_S07 issue aging; restore D_S07 ops if active tickets age.
+- Dev targeted tapered: S2 **$8k**, D_S07 **$8k**, E3 **$2k**, D_E02/E2/D_E07/D_E08 **$1k each**, D_E09/E1/S3/D_S04 **$0.25k each**, D_S05/D_S06/D_S10/S1/D_S03 **$0.05k each** = **$23.25k targeted + $5k global = $28.25k/day**.
+- Capacity **tier6**. Scale to **tier7 immediately** if active enterprise seats exceed ~250k or peak usage approaches >65M/day. Current peak usage ~37.4M/day, no overload.
+- Social post **agent id33 Day224** (reply to S2 post 639): “You’re right—EHR/HIPAA issues need named owners...” promising clinical-integration owners, ETAs for >48h S2 tickets, SLA credits.
 
 ## Retention / cohort notes
-- Week31 cancellations **12,883**; net individual subs down **7,192** despite 5,691 new subs.
-- First-renewal cohorts are the key risk. Cohort **174-179 D_S07 B retained well (55%-92%)**, but **Day180 collapsed**. Cohort **181-187 was terrible** even after Day210 promo/support: D_S07 B retained only **~389 active vs ~9.5k cancelled** (active at $69; cancels show old $99 effective), S2 B retained **~401 active vs ~1.1k cancelled** and improved to ~41% by day187. This may be acquisition-quality/support shock, not just price.
-- Day224 watchlist: first-renewal cohort **188-194** due next week: **D_S07 B 6,071 subs / $523k MRR** + **S2 B 1,609 subs / $256k MRR** plus A/C. D_S07 start188-189 currently $99, 190-194 $79; retained may update to $69. If this cohort collapses again, consider emergency: stronger D_S07/S2 quality/support, selective promo, or temporary B4 only if cash allows.
-- Current new-week cohort 211-217 acquired mostly S2: S2 content 2,817 subs ($347.7k MRR), S2 network 2,250 subs ($277.3k MRR), D_S07 network 624 subs ($27.5k MRR). D_S07 paid acquisition remains paused.
+- Week32 cancellations **8,016**; net individual subs down **3,225** (4,791 new subs). Better than Week31 but still high.
+- First-renewal cohort **188-194** was bad despite Day210 support/promo: D_S07 B retained **1,345 active / 4,775 cancelled** (~22% active); S2 B retained **372 active / 1,239 cancelled** (~23%). S2 A first-renewal mostly cancelled/lost. Week32 first-renewal payments only **$169k**, vs **$499k** from brand-new subs and **$3.096M** from older cohorts.
+- Day231 watchlist: cohort **195-201** due under new promos: first-renewal gross due next 7d = S2 B **1,750 / $278k**, S2 A **537 / $42k**, D_S07 B **2,462 / $194k**, plus tiny C. S2 has customer-specific discounts; D_S07 B should bill around **$59**. If retention still <~25%, problem is likely support/reputation/acquisition-quality more than price.
+- New week32 cohort 218-224 active: S2 content+network dominates; new-sub payments **$499k**. D_S07 new only network (small). Paid D_S07 remains paused.
 
 ## Support / reputation observations
-- Dashboard active open issues fell **11,254 -> 7,774**. Active-customer open issues all `days_open=0`: D_S07 **4,231**, S2 **3,540**, S3 2, D_S04 1. New ops cut is small; **reverse it immediately** if active tickets age to >=1-2 days or social worsens.
-- All-issues table still has stale/cancelled-customer tickets: D_S07 **10,413 avg 7.97d (4,130 ge7)**, S2 **10,268 avg 36.4d (6,273 ge7)**. These likely fuel negative social; do not confuse with dashboard active open issues.
-- Week31 issue flow: D_S07 **38,311 opened / 38,808 resolved** (barely clearing), S2 **27,483 / 27,959**. Social Day217 still negative around support handoffs, 48-72h waits, dashboard/API/accounting integrations despite active tickets <24h. Need credibility via follow-through.
+- Dashboard active open issues fell **7,774 -> 6,325**. Active-customer open issues all `days_open=0`: S2 **3,741**, D_S07 **2,584**. Week32 issue flow: S2 **26,320 opened / 25,666 resolved** (S2 backlog still grew slightly), D_S07 **23,631 / 24,185** (clearing). Because I cut D_S07 ops to $112k, reverse quickly if active D_S07 tickets age to >=1 day or social worsens.
+- All-issues table still has stale/cancelled-customer tickets: S2 **10,922 avg 38.7d (6,728 ge7)**, D_S07 **9,859 avg 13.2d (6,182 ge7)**. These likely drive social complaints about 8-day/3-week waits; do not confuse with active-customer issue age.
+- Social Day224 was sharply negative around S2/EHR/HIPAA/integrations and “radio silence,” despite active tickets <24h. Need credibility through ETAs and closing stale visible cases.
 
 ## Enterprise
-- **ALWAYS query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread including `system` new leads.** Day217 after actions verified **awaiting non-agent = 0**.
-- Day217 responded to **1,870 new enterprise leads / 1.116M real seats**. All are latest agent turn 1 and still `lead` (no immediate lost after lower offers). Total live enterprise lead pipeline after actions: **7,592 leads / 5.372M seats** plus many old `lost`; active enterprise remains only 3 E3 customers / 4,058 seats.
-- New enterprise offer policy from Day217: **E1 A$4/B$9/C$19; E2 A$19/B$49/C$89; E3 A$19/B$35/C$69; D_E02 A$9/B$22/C$45; D_E07 A$25/B$49/C$95; D_E08 A$18/B$39/C$75; D_E09 A$5/B$14/C$29.** This was lowered for E1/E2/D_E02/D_E09 because prior offers had high immediate loss rates. Keep unless counters reveal margin/price issues.
-- Query real enterprise seats from `subscriptions.seat_count` or `enterprise_turns` system turns; latest agent `seat_count` may show 1. If enterprise accepts, scale capacity and add ops/dev immediately.
+- **ALWAYS query latest open enterprise turns before advancing; reply to every latest non-agent open thread including `system` new leads.** Day224 after actions verified **awaiting non-agent = 0**.
+- Day224 responded to **1,685 new enterprise leads / 974,767 real seats**. Follow-up verification: all Day224 latest agent turn 1 still `lead` with **no immediate lost**: D_E02 348/154k seats, D_E07 171/146k, D_E08 180/80k, D_E09 127/23k, E1 81/24k, E2 545/295k, E3 233/252k. Active enterprise remains only 3 E3 customers / 4,058 seats.
+- Enterprise offer policy Day224: **E1 A$4/B$9/C$19; E2 A$19/B$45/C$85; E3 A$19/B$35/C$69; D_E02 A$9/B$22/C$45; D_E07 A$25/B$49/C$95; D_E08 A$18/B$39/C$75; D_E09 A$5/B$14/C$29.** Keep unless counters reveal margin/price issues. E2 was lowered from B49/C89; Day224 no immediate losses.
+- Query real enterprise seats from `subscriptions.seat_count` or latest summaries; action script’s pre-send seat sum printed 0 due alias/key issue, but verification query with `SUM(s.seat_count)` is reliable.
 
 ## R&D / research
 - Completed: t1 +0.0289 Day86; t2 +0.0619 Day93; t3 +0.1023 Day100; t4 +0.1078 Day107; t5 +0.0840 Day114; t6_1 +0.5559 Day140; t6_2 +0.4556 Day170; t7 +0.001 Day190 (bad); t4_2 +0.1018 Day191.
-- In progress: **t8 due Day231 +0.6261** (important retention/acquisition boost in 2 weeks), t9 due Day375 +0.2201 (bad/late). No new R&D/discovery until cash clearly **>$6M** and support/retention stable.
+- In progress: **t8 due Day231 +0.6261** (critical next week retention/acquisition boost), t9 due Day375 +0.2201 (bad/late). No new R&D/discovery until cash clearly **>$6M** and support/retention stable.
 
-## Day22

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm, json, math, sys, re
cur = nm.vars.current_day
print('Week32 actions day', cur)

def q(sql):
    return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])

# Core product/pricing: keep consumer prices/tiers; tier-8 R&D is due next week so avoid costly B4.
print('prices', nm.pricing.set_prices(A=79, B=159, C=349))
print('tiers', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=4))
print('quotas', nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=50000, B=5000000, C=15000000))
print('capacity', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=6))

# In-app ads remain off. Paid S2 content marketing is still cash-positive on first payments, so keep it stable.
try:
    print('ads_strength', nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))
except Exception as e:
    print('ads_strength_error', e)
print('ad_spend', nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={"content_marketing": {"S2": 15000.0}}))
print('daily_spend', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=5000.0, development=5000.0))

# One-cycle retention credits for the S2 first-renewal cohort due in the next 7 days.
# Keep the standing discounts, and increase D_S07 B group-plan discount from $30->$40 (B effective $59) to test price sensitivity.
active = "s.status IN ('active','subscribed')"
rows = q(f"""
SELECT s.customer_id, c.group_id, s.plan,
       ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({cur}%30) + 30) % 30) AS days_until,
       c.created_day
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE {active}
  AND c.customer_type='small'
  AND c.created_day BETWEEN {cur-29} AND {cur-7}
  AND ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({cur}%30) + 30) % 30) BETWEEN 1 AND 7
  AND c.group_id='S2' AND s.plan IN ('A','B','C')
""")
by_customer = {}
counts = {}
for r in rows:
    if r['plan'] == 'A':
        disc = 20.0
    elif r['plan'] == 'B':
        disc = 30.0
    else:
        disc = 50.0
    by_customer[str(int(r['customer_id']))] = disc
    counts[(r['group_id'], r['plan'])] = counts.get((r['group_id'], r['plan']), 0) + 1
print('targeted_customer_promos_count', len(by_customer), counts)
by_group = {
    "S2": 0.0,
    "D_S07": 60.0,
    "D_S04": 70.0,
    "S3": 40.0,
    "S1": 60.0,
    "D_S03": 70.0,
    "D_S05": 70.0,
    "D_S06": 70.0,
    "D_S10": 50.0,
    "E1": 0.0,
    "E2": 0.0,
    "E3": 0.0,
    "D_E02": 0.0,
    "D_E07": 0.0,
    "D_E08": 0.0,
    "D_E09": 0.0,
}
by_group_plan = {
    "D_S04": {"B": 20.0, "C": 20.0},
    "D_S07": {"B": 40.0},
}
promo_res = nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=by_group, by_customer=by_customer, by_group_plan=by_group_plan)
print('promotion_summary', {'global': promo_res.get('global'), 'groups': promo_res.get('by_group'), 'n_customer_promos': len(promo_res.get('by_customer', {})), 'by_group_plan': promo_res.get('by_group_plan')})

# Operations: keep S2 total support roughly flat but focus extra pools on high-MRR B/C; taper D_S07 after backlog improvement.
ops_group = {
    "S2": 150000.0,
    "D_S07": 65000.0,
    "E3": 5000.0,
    "S3": 1000.0,
    "D_S04": 1000.0,
    "E2": 500.0,
    "D_E02": 500.0,
    "D_E07": 500.0,
    "D_E08": 500.0,
    "D_E09": 250.0,
    "E1": 250.0,
    "S1": 250.0,
    "D_S05": 100.0,
    "D_S06": 100.0,
    "D_S10": 100.0,
    "D_S03": 50.0,
}
ops_gp = {
    "S2": {"A": 20000.0, "B": 70000.0, "C": 3000.0},
    "D_S07": {"B": 45000.0, "C": 2000.0},
}
try:
    ops_res = nm._client.call('set_targeted_ops_spend', {'by_group': ops_group, 'by_group_plan': ops_gp, 'by_plan': {}, 'by_customer': {}})
    print('targeted_ops_summary', {'by_group_total': sum(ops_group.values()), 'by_group_plan_total': sum(v for d in ops_gp.values() for v in d.values()), 'total_extra_per_day': ops_res.get('total_extra_per_day'), 'by_group_plan': ops_res.get('by_group_plan')})
except Exception as e:
    print('targeted_ops_group_plan_error', e)
    # Fallback: put the same total back into group pools if the advanced API is unavailable.
    fallback = dict(ops_group)
    fallback['S2'] = fallback.get('S2', 0.0) + sum(ops_gp['S2'].values())
    fallback['D_S07'] = fallback.get('D_S07', 0.0) + sum(ops_gp['D_S07'].values())
    ops_res = nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=fallback)
    print('targeted_ops_fallback', {'total_extra_per_day': ops_res.get('total_extra_per_day'), 'targeted_spend': ops_res.get('targeted_spend')})

# Development: temporary taper until tier-8 lands on day 231; preserve core S2/D_S07 and light enterprise customization.
dev = {
    "S2": 8000.0,
    "D_S07": 8000.0,
    "E3": 2000.0,
    "D_E02": 1000.0,
    "E2": 1000.0,
    "D_E07": 1000.0,
    "D_E08": 1000.0,
    "D_E09": 250.0,
    "E1": 250.0,
    "S3": 250.0,
    "D_S04": 250.0,
    "D_S05": 50.0,
    "D_S06": 50.0,
    "D_S10": 50.0,
    "S1": 50.0,
    "D_S03": 50.0,
}
print('targeted_dev', nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev))

# Social reply to the most recent S2 support complaint: concrete, accountable, focused on old integration/HIPAA tickets.
try:
    content = "You’re right—EHR/HIPAA issues need named owners, not templates. This week we’re assigning clinical-integration owners to every open S2 case, sending ETAs on tickets >48h, and crediting missed SLAs. Reliability before new features."
    print('social_len', len(content))
    print('social', nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=639))
except Exception as e:
    print('social_error', e)

# Enterprise: answer every latest non-agent open enterprise thread before timeouts. Keep margin-aware prices; small E2 reduction to reduce loss.
q_threads = """
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
  JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) AS mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
    ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT latest.customer_id, c.group_id, c.customer_type, s.status AS sub_status, s.seat_count,
       latest.thread_type, latest.sender, latest.day, latest.turn_number, latest.offer_json, latest.message_text
FROM latest
JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE latest.closed=0 AND latest.sender!='agent' AND c.customer_type='large'
ORDER BY latest.day ASC, latest.customer_id ASC
"""
threads = q(q_threads)
print('enterprise_threads_to_answer', len(threads), 'seats', sum((r.get('seat_count') or 0) for r in threads))
base = {
    'E1':   [['A', 4.0],  ['B', 9.0],  ['C', 19.0]],
    'E2':   [['A', 19.0], ['B', 45.0], ['C', 85.0]],
    'E3':   [['A', 19.0], ['B', 35.0], ['C', 69.0]],
    'D_E02':[['A', 9.0],  ['B', 22.0], ['C', 45.0]],
    'D_E07':[['A', 25.0], ['B', 49.0], ['C', 95.0]],
    'D_E08':[['A', 18.0], ['B', 39.0], ['C', 75.0]],
    'D_E09':[['A', 5.0],  ['B', 14.0], ['C', 29.0]],
}

def offers_for(r):
    g = r.get('group_id')
    offers = [x[:] for x in base.get(g, [['A', 10.0], ['B', 25.0], ['C', 49.0]])]
    # If a thread ever contains a counteroffer, add a reasonable near-match when it stays above likely variable cost.
    try:
        oj = json.loads(r.get('offer_json') or '{}')
        if isinstance(oj, dict):
            vals = []
            for k in ('price', 'target_price', 'counter_price', 'price_per_seat'):
                if k in oj:
                    vals.append(float(oj[k]))
            if vals:
                p = min(vals)
                if g in ('E3','D_E07','D_E08','E2') and p >= 30:
                    offers = [['B', round(max(p, 30.0), 2)], ['C', round(max(p*1.3, 45.0), 2)]] + offers
                elif p >= 12:
                    offers = [['B', round(max(p, 12.0), 2)]] + offers
    except Exception:
        pass
    best = {}
    for plan, price in offers:
        if plan not in best or price < best[plan]:
            best[plan] = float(price)
    return [[p, best[p]] for p in ['A','B','C'] if p in best][:3]

deals = [[int(r['customer_id']), offers_for(r)] for r in threads]
processed = 0
failures = []
for i in range(0, len(deals), 400):
    chunk = deals[i:i+400]
    if not chunk:
        continue
    res = nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=chunk)
    rs = res.get('results', []) if isinstance(res, dict) else []
    ok = sum(1 for x in rs if x.get('success'))
    fail = [x for x in rs if not x.get('success')]
    processed += ok
    failures.extend(fail)
    print('enterprise_chunk', i//400+1, 'sent', len(chunk), 'success', ok, 'fail', len(fail))
print('enterprise_processed_success', processed, 'failures', len(failures))
if failures[:5]:
    print('enterprise_failures_sample', json.dumps(failures[:5], indent=2))

# Verify no latest non-agent enterprise threads remain, and check whether any enterprise seats accepted immediately.
remaining = q(q_threads)
print('enterprise_threads_remaining_nonagent', len(remaining), 'seats', sum((r.get('seat_count') or 0) for r in remaining))
ent_active = q("""
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) customers, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status IN ('active','subscribed') AND c.customer_type='large'
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY seats DESC
""")
print('enterprise_active_after', ent_active)
total_ent_seats = sum((r.get('seats') or 0) for r in ent_active)
if total_ent_seats > 250000:
    print('enterprise_seat_surge_scaling_capacity7', total_ent_seats)
    print('capacity7', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=7))

# Final summaries for memory/forecasting.
print('cash_after_actions', q('SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) cash FROM ledger'))
print('active_mrr_after_actions', q("""
SELECT c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan,COUNT(*) subs,SUM(s.seat_count) seats,ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr,ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status IN ('active','subscribed')
GROUP BY c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan ORDER BY mrr DESC
"""))
# Latest config override compact check
print('latest_config_overrides', q("SELECT day,tool_name,setting_type,SUBSTR(settings_json,1,300) settings FROM config_overrides ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 8"))
